methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $135M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Chai Discovery

private.name: Eikon Therapeutics

data.narrative:
SpliceBio is a Series B-stage biotech & health company founded by Miquel Vila-Perell track record of $135M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment benchmarks against a peer universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers, including same-stage companies like Chai Discovery ($3.8B) and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M), as well as adjacent-stage performers like Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies $256M. The peer-median approach across 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $3.5B, with a range spanning $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.9x) is not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue. 

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $218M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around SpliceBio's relative maturity within the peer cohort and stage-specific risk factors. Stage-progression yields a more conservative floor, while peer-median anchoring reflects the upper-end valuation environment in frontier biotech. Revenue visibility and clinical milestone progression remain primary drivers of valuation resolution within this range.
